rulings.Case Title: BETTY UTTERBACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 11, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT BAYER CROPSCIENCE, INC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Defendant did not shift the burden of producing evidence in that the Sage declaration does not demonstrate that plaintiff is unable to prove its case. The declarations of Mr. Utterback and Mr. Ay are admitted and create a triable issue of fact as well. Summary adjudication is granted as to the false representation and punitive damages claim. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: NICHOLAS RENAULT VS. CHRISTOPHER ARMENTROUT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reclassify Action To The Court Of Limited Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 11, 2008, line 5. DEFENDANT CHRISTOPHER ARMENTROUT'S Motion To Reclassify Action To The Court Of Limited Jurisdiction is granted. It is not reasonably probable that a verdict would exceed $25,000 based on the facts presented. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: EMMANUEL ADDO VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 11, 2008, line 6. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. The Court takes judicial notice of the administrative record. It does not appear that plaintiff has exhausted the administrative process and that plaintiff's remedy is to take a writ from that proceeding. Gupta v. Stanford University (2004) 124 Cal.App. 4th 407. Plaintiff's futility argument may be applicable to a writ proceeding but it does not create any of the remedies plaintiff has alleged in this case. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 11, 2008, line 9. DEFENDANTS 1596 CHURCH STREET CONDOMINIUMS, LLC, MARC HANNAH, JAMES BRANCH AND J. BRANCH DEVELOPMENTS, INC'S Special Motion To Strike (CCP 425.16, Anti-Slapp Statue) is off calendar - insufficient notice. CCP 1005 (b) requires 16 court days, plus 5 calendar days for service by mail. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: SFP SOUTH PARK LLC VS. ALTA PACIFIC VENTURES LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause For A Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 11, 2008, line 10. PLAINTIFF'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. The motion is denied. Plaintiff appears to have an adequate remedy at law and plaintiff's showing of irreparable harm is speculative. On the question of probable success, it appears that both parties may have breached the agreement. By failing to seek mediation and by entering into contracts of sale, plaintiff has created the present emergency, at least, in part. Given the present record, the Court cannot say that plaintiff is more likely to succeed or is more likely to sustain irreparable harm. =(302/PJM/PB)

By failing to seek mediation and by entering into contracts of sale, plaintiff has created the present emergency, at least, in part. Given the present record, the Court cannot say that plaintiff is more likely to succeed or is more likely to sustain irreparable harm. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: GRANT KWOK et al VS. KENNETH K.W. NG et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 11, 2008, line 14. PLAINTIFFS GRANT KWOK, MICHAEL KWONG AND KENDRIC KWOK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff has shifted the burden of producing evidence by demonstrating compliance with the Ellis Act. Defendant creates a triable issue of fact by asserting the rental value is excessive. As a consequence, there are triable issues of fact as to facts 16 and 17. =(302/PJM)
